title: 控方证人
summary:
On a garden path, Charlotte Dinsmoor confesses her fear to Mortimer Cleveland, stating that her mother, father, and sister, Maggie, have all changed since moving into their house. She notes the house is rumored to be haunted because a man murdered his wife there in the past. Cleveland professionally suggests that Charlotte may be a sensitive individual acting as a subconscious medium; he proposes that she might have written the "SOS" message the previous night by unconsciously reenacting the actions of the former victim. After Charlotte leaves to join her family, Cleveland reflects on the household's atmosphere, noting that Johnny is the only family member who does not seem afraid.

During breakfast, Cleveland observes that Johnny has stained fingers, which the boy attributes to his interest in chemistry research. This interest angers Mr. Dinsmoor, who wants Johnny to enter the building trade. While Mr. Dinsmoor appears jovial, he harbors animosity toward Cleveland, and Mrs. Dinsmoor greets him coldly while appearing afraid of him. When Maggie asks Cleveland if he slept well, he gives a polite response that leaves her looking disappointed. When Cleveland mentions Johnny's interest in chemistry, Mrs. Dinsmoor reacts by suddenly dropping her teacup, causing it to crash.

Later, while walking in the garden, Cleveland overhears Mr. Dinsmoor discussing a fortune of nearly six thousand pounds mentioned by a lawyer. This revelation regarding money makes the situation feel more "ugly" to Cleveland. Despite Mr. Dinsmoor's warm greeting, Cleveland feels a desire to leave the house and thanks him for his hospitality.
